columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier; primary key and merge key for incremental loads alongside updated_date

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee first name; sourced from varchar(50)

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Employee last name; sourced from varchar(50)

columns.name: email
columns.description: Employee email address; sourced from varchar(100)

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number; nullable in source; sourced from varchar(20)

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role identifier; low-to-medium cardinality; third clustering column

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee base salary; precision 8 scale 2 preserved via NUMERIC

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage; precision 4 scale 2; nullable in source

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referencing FK to employee_id representing reporting manager; NULL for top-level employees

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier; nullable in source; first clustering column supporting departmental analytics

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employee status code (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE); very low cardinality; second clustering column

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp; MySQL TIMESTAMP maps directly to BigQuery TIMESTAMP (UTC)

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Record last updated timestamp; partition column (DAY granularity) and merge key for incremental loads; NULL rows land in __NULL__ partition

cardinality.notes: Many employees report to one manager via manager_id → employee_id self-join; top-level employees carry NULL manager_id
